title: price_volume_trend.py output
description: price_volume_trend.py requires --component. component_breakdown is one row per calendar month (order_month), the same monthly avg_unit_price/total_qty series plotted in the chart. criticality_breakdown is the same, split further by component_criticality. avg_unit_price here is intentionally an UNWEIGHTED mean of unit_cost_usd per month — unlike every other operation's avg_unit_price/avg_price fields, this is a deliberate choice for a trend line (equal weight per transaction reveals quoted-price movement over time; spend-weighting would answer a different question and could mask price creep behind a few large low-priced orders).

order_month.description: First day of the month, e.g. "2025-03-01".

avg_unit_price.description: Unweighted mean of unit_cost_usd for the month — see top-level description.

component_breakdown.description: One row per order_month in the filtered scope. Same series the chart plots.

criticality_breakdown.description: One row per (order_month, component_criticality) combination present in scope.
